privileged_info_summary: Task involves organizing four foundational AI papers in Zotero: (1) Turing's 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' (Mind, 1950) which introduces the Imitation Game test—a note will be attached mentioning this key concept; (2) Turing's 'On Computable Numbers' (London Mathematical Society, 1936); (3) Shannon's 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' (Bell System Technical Journal, 1948); and (4) McCarthy's 'Recursive Functions of Symbolic Expressions' (Communications of the ACM, 1960). The collection will be named 'AI History Seminar' and a report will be exported to '/home/ga/Documents/ai_seminar_report.html'. All paper metadata is accurate—titles, authors, years, and publication venues correspond to real historical papers.
